analyze_vdj_batch.description:
V(D)J recombination analysis using IgBLAST + ChangeO.
    Returns AIRR format results.
    
    Automatically splits large files into batches for efficient processing.
    
    NOTE: For CSV input, the system will automatically convert to FASTA format.
    Supported CSV column names:
    - BCR (antibody): Heavy_DNA, Light_DNA, heavy_dna, light_dna, Heavy, Light, sequence, VH, VL, etc.
    - TCR (T cell receptor): alpha_dna, beta_dna, TRA, TRB, CDR3a, CDR3b, tra_seq, trb_seq, etc.

    Args:
        args: AnalyzeVdjBatchArgs - Parameters for V(D)J analysis

    Returns:
        AIRR format results from ChangeO

input_params.name: sequences
input_params.description: Sequences input: (1) FASTA file path (.fasta/.fa), (2) CSV file path with sequence columns (auto-converted to FASTA). For BCR: Heavy_DNA/Light_DNA, Heavy/Light, sequence columns. For TCR: alpha_dna/beta_dna, TRA/TRB, CDR3a/CDR3b columns.

input_params.name: organism
input_params.description: Organism type for germline database. Supported: human, mouse, rhesus_monkey

input_params.name: receptor_type
input_params.description: Receptor type: 'Ig' for BCR (antibody) or 'TCR' for T cell receptor. Use 'Ig' for IGH/IGK/IGL loci, 'TCR' for TRA/TRB/TRG/TRD loci.

input_params.name: locus
input_params.description: Locus type. For BCR: IGH, IGK, IGL. For TCR: TRA, TRB, TRG, TRD.

input_params.name: timeout
input_params.description: Timeout in seconds for IgBLAST and ChangeO execution. IMPORTANT: This must be an INTEGER value (e.g., 7200), NOT a string. Default: 7200 seconds (2 hours). Increase for very large files.

extract_cdr3_from_airr.description:
Extract CDR3 information from AIRR format results.
    
    Supports multiple input formats:
    - Array of AIRR records: [{"sequence_id": "...", "junction": "...", ...}, ...]
    - Local file path: "/path/to/airr_results.csv" or "/path/to/airr_results.json"
    - HTTP/HTTPS URL: "https://example.com/airr_results.csv"
    
    通过 SSE 流式推送提取进度，支持大文件处理。

    Args:
        args: ExtractCdr3FromAirrArgs - Parameters for CDR3 extraction

    Yields:
        Progress updates and final result through SSE stream.

input_params.name: airr_results
input_params.description: AIRR format results from V(D)J analysis. Can be: (1) Array of AIRR records, or (2) Local file path (CSV/TSV/JSON). For URLs, please use the File Utils service to download first.

integrate_binding_neutralization_experim.description:
Integrate antibody binding and neutralization experimental measurements
    
    This tool corresponds to the functionality in notebook 3.5.clone_result.ipynb.
    Performs the following data processing steps:
    
    **Experimental Data Standardization**
    - Load two batches of antibody functional assay data
    - Apply thresholds to convert continuous measurements into binary labels (binding+/-, neutralization+/-)
    - Process binding and neutralization data for multiple influenza strains (H1N1, H3N2)
    - Standardize antibody nomenclature and batch information
    
    **Data Integration**

    - Prioritize results from more recent batches

integrate_predictions_with_experimental_.description:
Integrate machine learning prediction results with laboratory measurements
    
    This tool corresponds to the functionality in notebook 4.experiment+prediction.ipynb.
    Performs the following data integration workflow:
    
    **Machine Learning Prediction Data**
    - Load ensemble prediction results from multiple folds
    - Binding predictions: MetaBCR model predictions for H1N1/H3N2 strain binding
    - Neutralization predictions: Multi-fold cross-validated neutralization activity predictions
    - Pivot prediction scores into wide-format tables
    
    **Experimental Measurement Data**
    - Load laboratory-measured binding and neutralization activities
    - Standardize experimental results into binary classifications
    
    **Data Merging**
    - Merge prediction and experimental data into BCR feature dataset
    - Add single-cell/bulk data type labels
    - Retain all BCR sequence features, UMAP coordinates, and cell types

metabcr.description:
MetaBCR: A Deep Learning Framework for Antibody-Antigen Interaction Prediction

    It supports multiple model architectures, including CNN, GNN, and BERT-based models, and can be configured
    for various tasks and datasets through command-line arguments and configuration files.

        Progress updates and final result through SSE stream.

differential_gene_expression_correlation.description:
Integrated pipeline: differential gene expression volcano analysis + correlation analysis
    
    This pipeline automatically orchestrates two sequential bioinformatics analyses:
    
    **Step 1: Volcano Plot Analysis**
    - Identifies differentially expressed genes between broad reactive and specific B cells
    - Uses Seurat FindMarkers with 'both' strategy to generate two DEG lists
    - Applies smart threshold setting based on data distribution
    - Generates volcano plot with significant gene annotations
    - Exports two DEG CSV files (broad_control and specific_control)
    
    **Step 2: Correlation Analysis**
    - Automatically extracts the two DEG files from step 1 as input
    - Validates DEG file format and required columns (avg_log2FC, p_val_adj)
    - Filters significant genes using p-value threshold
    - Computes Pearson correlation coefficient between the two datasets
    - Generates correlation scatter plot with highlighted genes
    - Exports correlation statistics and detailed correlation data
    
    **Key Features:**
    - Seamless data flow: DEG files automatically passed between steps
    - Ensures statistical significance with minimum common genes requirement
    - Comprehensive output: Returns all 6 files from both analyses
    - Detailed execution summary with step-by-step status
    
    Bioinformatics domains: ["single-cell", "differential expression", "volcano plot", "correlation analysis", "statistics analysis", "automated pipeline"]
    Input data: ["Single-cell RNA-seq RDS files", "gene expression matrix", "cell type annotations"]
    Output results: ["Volcano plot PDF", "Two DEG CSV files", "Correlation scatter plot PDF", "Correlation statistics CSV", "Correlation data CSV"]
    
    Args:
        args: DifferentialGeneCorrelationPipelineArgs - Parameters for the integrated pipeline
        
    Yields:
        Progress updates and final result through SSE stream.

input_params.name: input_file
input_params.description: Complete path to input RDS file containing gene expression data

input_params.description: Absolute path to output directory for saving analysis results

integrate_bcr_data_complete.description:
Complete BCR data integration pipeline with UMAP, clustering, and cell type annotation.
    
    This advanced tool performs comprehensive integration of BCR prediction data with single-cell
    RNA-seq data, including:
    - Automatic detection and conversion of Excel files
    - Intelligent field version control (protects Heavy/Light chains, versions prediction fields)
    - UMAP dimensionality reduction and visualization
    - FindClusters for cell clustering analysis
    - Marker gene-based cell type annotation with confidence scoring
    
    The tool automatically handles:
    - Excel (.xlsx, .xls) to CSV conversion
    - Field standardization and barcode matching
    - Smart version control for repeated integrations
    - Complete B-cell subset annotation (Naive, Memory, Plasma, GC, etc.)
    
    Use cases:
    - One-click BCR prediction data integration
    - Complete single-cell B-cell analysis pipeline
    - Version-controlled data updates
    - Reproducible B-cell immunology analysis
    
    Args:
        args: IntegrateAllArgs containing all parameters
        
    Yields:
        Progress updates and final result through SSE stream.
        
    Example:
        integrate_bcr_data_complete(IntegrateAllArgs(
            csv_file="/data/predictions.xlsx",
            rds_file="/data/Age_Bcells.rds",

    Performs comprehensive trajectory and temporal analysis on B-cell differentiation:

使用 SPIRED-Fitness 进行蛋白质结构适应度评分和稳定性预测
    
    Args:
        input_path: 输入文件路径(.fasta) 或 包含fasta文件的文件夹路径
        output_dir: 结果输出目录路径

foldx_saturation_scan.description:
[FoldX] 饱和突变扫描 / 单点突变。

    功能：支持 CIF/PDB 输入，自动 RepairPDB，生成 individual_list，执行 BuildModel，
    输出 CSV（含 wt_total_energy, mutant_total_energy, dif_total_energy）。

    重要：input_pdb 必须是蛋白质结构文件（.cif 或 .pdb），不接受 CSV、FASTA 等文件。
    此工具与 SPIRED-Fitness 完全独立，应并行运行。

    参数：
        input_pdb: CIF 或 PDB 文件路径（必须是结构文件，不接受 CSV/FASTA 等）。
        position: 突变方式：
            - "117M"：单点突变，将 117 位突变为 M（适用于单体和多聚体）。
            - "satu"：全基因饱和突变，每位点突变为其余 19 种氨基酸。
        output_dir: 结果输出目录。

combine_and_filter.description:
整合 FoldX 和 SPIRED-Fitness 结果并筛选最佳突变。

    功能：
    1. 智能查找 foldx_saturation_scan 输出的 *saturation*.csv 和 SPIRED 输出的 single_mut_pred.csv
       （支持从 session 输出目录树中自动搜索，即使参数路径不完全准确也能正确找到文件）
    2. 按突变位置匹配，将 pred_score 添加到 FoldX 结果中
    3. 根据突变信息和 WT 序列生成突变后的氨基酸序列 (mut_seq 列)
    4. 筛选：删除 pred_score < 0 和 dif_total_energy > 0 的行
    5. 取 pred_score 最大 top X% 和 dif_total_energy 最小 top X% 的交集
    6. 按 pred_score 降序、dif_total_energy 升序排序输出

    参数：
        foldx_scan_dir: FoldX saturation scan 相关路径（目录、*saturation*.csv 文件路径，
                        或 session 输出树中的任意路径，工具会自动搜索正确文件）
        fitness_scan_dir: SPIRED-Fitness 相关路径（目录、single_mut_pred.csv 文件路径，
                         或 session 输出树中的任意路径，工具会自动搜索正确文件）
        wt_fasta_path: 原始未突变蛋白的 FASTA 文件路径
        output_dir: 输出目录
        top_percent: 筛选百分比（默认 10，即取 top 10%）

    返回：
        dict: 包含状态、输出文件路径、统计信息
            - combined_csv: 整合后的完整 CSV（添加了 mut_seq 和 pred_score 列）
            - filtered_csv: 筛选后的最佳突变 CSV
            - combined_count: 整合后行数
            - filtered_count: 筛选后行数
            - wt_seq_length: WT 序列长度

infer_cdr1_cdr2_from_vgene.description:
Infer CDR1 and CDR2 sequences from TCR V gene names using IMGT reference data.
    
    CDR1 and CDR2 are germline-encoded regions that can be directly inferred from V gene names.
    This is particularly useful when you only have V gene names and CDR3 sequences but need
    the full CDR1-3 set for NetTCR prediction.
    
    **SCIENTIFIC BACKGROUND:**
    - CDR1 and CDR2 are encoded within the V gene germline sequence
    - For TCRs, these regions do NOT undergo somatic mutation (unlike BCR/antibodies)
    - Therefore, the same V gene always produces the same CDR1 and CDR2 sequences
    - We use IMGT *01 allele sequences by default when allele info is not specified
    
    **SUPPORTED V GENE FORMATS:**
    - TRAV1-1, TRAV1-1*01, TRAV1-1*02 (alpha chain)
    - TRBV12-1, TRBV12-1*01, TRBV12-1*02 (beta chain)
    
    Args:
        tra_v_gene: TRAV gene name for alpha chain
        trb_v_gene: TRBV gene name for beta chain
        cdr3a: CDR3 alpha chain sequence (A3)
        cdr3b: CDR3 beta chain sequence (B3)
        peptide: Target peptide sequence
    
    Returns:
        Formatted output with inferred CDR1/CDR2 sequences in NetTCR format (A1, A2, A3, B1, B2, B3)

input_params.name: tra_v_gene
input_params.description: TRAV gene name for alpha chain (e.g., 'TRAV1-1', 'TRAV1-1*01')

input_params.name: trb_v_gene
input_params.description: TRBV gene name for beta chain (e.g., 'TRBV12-1', 'TRBV12-1*01')

input_params.name: cdr3a
input_params.description: CDR3 alpha chain amino acid sequence (A3 in NetTCR format)

input_params.name: cdr3b
input_params.description: CDR3 beta chain amino acid sequence (B3 in NetTCR format)

input_params.name: peptide
input_params.description: Target peptide sequence for binding prediction

convert_tcr_to_nettcr_format.description:
Convert TCR data with V gene names to NetTCR format CSV.
    
    This tool reads a CSV file containing TCR V gene names and CDR3 sequences, infers
    CDR1 and CDR2 from the V gene names using IMGT reference data, and outputs a CSV
    file in NetTCR format ready for prediction.
    
    **WHEN TO USE:**
    Use this tool when your data has V gene column names (e.g., TRA_v_gene, TRB_v_gene)
    but only CDR3 sequences. It will automatically infer CDR1 and CDR2.
    
    **INPUT COLUMN AUTO-DETECTION:**
    The function auto-detects these column patterns (case-insensitive):
    - V gene columns: TRA_v_gene, TRAV, v_a_gene, alpha_v, TRB_v_gene, TRBV, v_b_gene, beta_v
    - CDR3 columns: CDR3a, cdr3a, CDR3_alpha, alpha_cdr3, CDR3b, cdr3b, CDR3_beta, beta_cdr3
    
    **OUTPUT FORMAT (NetTCR):**
    - A1: CDR1 alpha (inferred from TRAV gene)
    - A2: CDR2 alpha (inferred from TRAV gene)
    - A3: CDR3 alpha (from input)
    - B1: CDR1 beta (inferred from TRBV gene)
    - B2: CDR2 beta (inferred from TRBV gene)
    - B3: CDR3 beta (from input)
    - peptide: Target peptide sequence
    
    Args:
        input_data: Path to input CSV file
        peptide: Target peptide sequence (same for all rows)
        tra_vgene_column: Column name for TRAV gene (auto-detected if not specified)
        trb_vgene_column: Column name for TRBV gene (auto-detected if not specified)
        cdr3a_column: Column name for CDR3 alpha (auto-detected if not specified)
        cdr3b_column: Column name for CDR3 beta (auto-detected if not specified)
        id_column: Column name for row identifier (e.g., 'main_name', 'id')
        output_path: Output file path (default: same directory as input with '_nettcr_format' suffix)
    
    Returns: Conversion summary with output file path and statistics

input_params.name: input_data
input_params.description: REQUIRED: CSV file path containing TCR data with V gene columns. The tool will auto-detect column names.

input_params.name: peptide
input_params.description: Target peptide sequence for binding prediction (e.g., 'ELAGIGILTV' for MART-1)

input_params.name: tra_vgene_column
input_params.description: Column name for TRAV gene. Auto-detected if not specified. Patterns: TRA_v_gene, TRAV, v_a_gene, alpha_v

input_params.name: trb_vgene_column
input_params.description: Column name for TRBV gene. Auto-detected if not specified. Patterns: TRB_v_gene, TRBV, v_b_gene, beta_v

input_params.name: cdr3a_column
input_params.description: Column name for CDR3 alpha. Auto-detected if not specified. Patterns: CDR3a, cdr3a, CDR3_alpha

input_params.name: cdr3b_column
input_params.description: Column name for CDR3 beta. Auto-detected if not specified. Patterns: CDR3b, cdr3b, CDR3_beta

input_params.name: id_column

input_params.name: output_path

input_params.name: output_dir
input_params.description: Output directory for the converted file. Use this to save output to sandbox directory (e.g., /data/sessions/{session_id}/output). Output filename will be auto-generated from input filename with '_nettcr_format.csv' suffix.

predict_tcr_binding_fast.description: Fast TCR-peptide binding prediction using pre-trained NetTCR-2.2 models. Uses a 20-model ensemble of TFLite models for instant prediction. Supports 26 peptide-specific models with percentile rank rescaling, plus pan-specific fallback for unseen peptides. Auto-detects input format (native: peptide/A1-A3/B1-B3, or legacy: CDR3a/CDR3b/TRA/TRB_v_gene). Reference: Montemurro et al., NAR 2024.

input_params.description: Path to test CSV file with TCR sequences. Accepts native format (peptide, A1-A3, B1-B3) or legacy format (CDR3a, CDR3b, peptide, TRA_v_gene, TRB_v_gene)

predict_tcr_binding_complete.description: Complete NetTCR-2.2 prediction pipeline with statistics and report. Full pipeline: validates input -> resolves CDR1/CDR2 if needed -> runs 20-model ensemble -> computes percentile rank -> classifies binders -> outputs prediction CSV, statistics CSV, and summary report.

Peptides without models fall back to pan-specific prediction. Pre-trained peptides support percentile rank rescaling.

Pre-trained peptides have peptide-specific models and support percentile rank. Also shows germline CDR1/CDR2 lookup coverage (TRAV/TRBV genes).
